tasks.name: _help:detail
tasks.desc: Show detailed help for a command

tasks.name: _new:cmd
tasks.summary:
Create a new command directory under cmds/ with a Taskfile.yaml and
a .sh script skeleton. Prompts for name and description if omitted.
Use colons for subcommands: `new cmd NAME=deploy:prod` creates a
deploy:prod subcommand inside an existing `deploy` command.

Examples:
  kube new cmd
  kube new cmd NAME=build DESC="Build the project"
  kube new cmd NAME=deploy:prod DESC="Deploy to prod"

tasks.name: completion:bash
tasks.summary:
Emit a bash completion script for this CLI to stdout. Source it
from your shell rc, or pipe into a completions directory.

Examples:
  kube completion bash > ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/kube
  source <(kube completion bash)

tasks.name: completion:zsh
tasks.summary:
Emit a zsh completion script for this CLI to stdout. Write to any
directory in $fpath.

Examples:
  kube completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_kube"

tasks.name: config:edit
tasks.summary:
Open .clift.yaml in $EDITOR. Falls back to $VISUAL, then `vi`.

Examples:
  kube config edit

tasks.name: config:get
tasks.summary:
Print the value of a single key from .clift.yaml. Exits nonzero if
the key is absent.

Examples:
  kube config get log_theme

tasks.name: config:set
tasks.summary:
Set a key=value pair in .clift.yaml.

Examples:
  kube config set log_theme=icons-mono

tasks.name: config:show
tasks.summary:
Print the CLI's current configuration — log theme, version, and any
values set via `config set`. Reads from .clift.yaml.

Examples:
  kube config show
  kube config dump

tasks.name: config:theme
tasks.summary:
Interactively pick a log theme (affects log.sh output formatting).
Previews each option before committing.

Examples:
  kube config theme

tasks.name: deploy:default
tasks.summary:
Deploy services to the specified target environment.

Supports deploying multiple tagged images with configurable replica counts.
Use --force to skip the confirmation prompt.

Examples:
  kube deploy --target prod --tag=api:v1.2 --tag=web:v1.3
  kube dep -t staging --replicas 5
  kube deploy --force --tag=api:latest

tasks.name: deploy:rollback
tasks.summary:
Roll back the most recent deployment in the target environment.

Examples:
  kube deploy rollback --target prod
  kube deploy rollback -t staging

tasks.name: logs:default
tasks.summary:
View logs for a specific service.

Examples:
  kube logs --service api
  kube logs -s worker --lines 100
  kube logs -s api --follow

tasks.name: restart:default
tasks.desc: Restart a deployment (wraps kubectl rollout restart + status)
tasks.summary:
Restart a deployment and wait for the rollout to finish. Wraps two
kubectl invocations — rollout restart, then rollout status — behind
a single gum-spinner UX so the common case is one simple command
instead of a multi-step recipe.

Examples:
  kube restart web
  kube restart api --namespace staging
  kube restart worker --timeout 2m

tasks.name: status:default
tasks.summary:
Display the current deployment status for an environment.

Examples:
  kube status
  kube status --target prod --wide

tasks.name: update:default
tasks.summary:
Pull the latest clift framework into the directory pointed to by
$FRAMEWORK_DIR. Your CLI's cmds/ and config are not touched.

Examples:
  kube update
